body.title: Vetinari: post success signal to Akien when directive completes
body.result: _notify_directive_complete() posts VETINARI_COMPLETE; fire_completion guard idempotent; 5 tests
body.intention: I intend that Vetinari posts a structured completion message to the Akien channel when all child tickets for a directive are closed.
body.description:
Currently _escalate_to_akien() fires only on health degradation (bad news). The directive vision requires a success signal when work is done.

Extend check_directive_progress(): when status transitions to 'completed' for the first time (completed_at not set), call _notify_directive_complete(directive_id). _notify_directive_complete() posts: 'VETINARI_COMPLETE directive=<id> tickets=<N> summary=<text>' to channel and sets directive state completed_at. Mark directive archived in flat-file (not deleted — audit trail).

**Affected files:** devices/vetinari/device.py (add _notify_directive_complete(), extend check_directive_progress()), tests/test_vetinari_signal.py (NEW)
**Design rules:** idempotent — completion fires exactly once per directive (guard on completed_at); channel post via _channel_post (existing)
**Scope boundary:** IN — completion detection, VETINARI_COMPLETE post, directive archive; OUT — re-open path (out of scope for this ticket)
**Completion criteria:** when all child tickets close and check_directive_progress() is called: VETINARI_COMPLETE posted to channel; directive state has completed_at; second call does NOT re-post; 4+ tests
